title: rosa canina subvar. oenensis
intro: Rosa canina subvar. oenensis, also known as the Dog Rose, is a species of rose native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible hips. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subvar. oenensis include its high vitamin C content, flavonoids, and essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Rosa canina subvar. oenensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and jams, as well as being used in traditional medicine and skincare products.
